title: Concurrent evaluate() with async fan-out
analysis:
Task 29 adds concurrent evaluation to the Engine, where independent nodes within a topological level fan out to parallel tokio tasks. The foundation is already in place from tasks 25-27: reify-runtime has ConcurrentScheduler (level-based async execution), AsyncNodeEvaluator trait (immutable-ref async evaluation), and CancellationToken. The Engine in reify-eval is currently fully synchronous with a sequential edit_param() loop.

The key integration challenge is bridging Engine's mutable state (cache, journal, snapshot, values) with the concurrent scheduler's shared-immutable model. Engine holds Box<dyn ConstraintChecker> and other non-Send types, making it impossible to share via Arc directly.

Design approach: (1) Engine exposes prepare_concurrent_edit() which clones/extracts state into a ConcurrentEditSetup value type (cheap via PersistentMap O(1) clones), and apply_concurrent_edit() to merge results back. (2) A ConcurrentEvalAdapter in reify-runtime implements AsyncNodeEvaluator using interior mutability (Arc<RwLock<ValueMap>> for shared values, Arc<RwLock<HashSet>> for skip tracking). (3) Content hash comparison uses pre-extracted previous hashes from CacheStore, avoiding shared mutable cache during concurrent eval. (4) Results are batch-applied to cache/journal/snapshot after concurrent execution completes. (5) Early cutoff propagates between levels via a shared skip set checked by is_dirty(). This design keeps reify-eval free of tokio dependency while enabling full concurrency in reify-runtime.

Existing ConcurrentScheduler already handles: level computation via Kahn's algorithm, spawning tokio tasks per level, joining before next level, cancellation checking between levels, and SchedulerError propagation for panics. The adapter just needs to implement the two-method AsyncNodeEvaluator trait.

prerequisites.description: Add Clone derive to ReverseDependencyIndex in reify-eval/src/deps.rs (inner HashMap<ValueCellId, HashSet<NodeId>> is already Clone-able, just needs the derive). This enables cheap cloning for the concurrent eval setup.

Update reify-runtime/Cargo.toml: add reify-expr.workspace = true to [dependencies] (needed for eval_expr in adapter). Move reify-compiler.workspace = true from [dev-dependencies] to [dependencies] (needed for CompiledExpr, ValueCellKind types).

Define ConcurrentEditSetup struct in reify-eval/src/lib.rs containing all state needed for concurrent evaluation: eval_set (Vec<NodeId>), graph (EvaluationGraph), values (ValueMap), snapshot_values (PersistentMap<ValueCellId, (Value, DeterminacyState)>), traces (HashMap<NodeId, DependencyTrace>), reverse_index (ReverseDependencyIndex), previous_hashes (HashMap<NodeId, ContentHash>), version (VersionId), snapshot_id (SnapshotId), parent_snapshot_id (SnapshotId), changed_cells (HashSet<ValueCellId>).

Define ConcurrentNodeResult struct {node: NodeId, value: Value, determinacy: DeterminacyState, trace: DependencyTrace, outcome: EvalOutcome} and ConcurrentEditResult struct {values: ValueMap, snapshot_values: PersistentMap<ValueCellId, (Value, DeterminacyState)>, node_results: Vec<ConcurrentNodeResult>, actual_eval_set: Vec<NodeId>, skipped: HashSet<NodeId>} in reify-eval/src/lib.rs.

steps.description: Write failing test in reify-eval tests/concurrent.rs: Engine::prepare_concurrent_edit() returns ConcurrentEditSetup with correct state. Use bracket topology: eval(), then prepare_concurrent_edit(width, new_value). Verify: (1) eval_set matches sequential dirty∩demand set (volume + C1 for width change), (2) previous_hashes contains entries for nodes in eval_set that had cache entries, (3) values map has all current parameter values, (4) graph.value_cells.len() == 6, (5) version is bumped from initial.

Implement Engine::prepare_concurrent_edit(cell: ValueCellId, new_value: Value) -> ConcurrentEditSetup. Clone snapshot (O(1) via PersistentMap), update changed cell value, compute dirty cone via compute_dirty_cone, compute eval set via compute_eval_set, extract previous content hashes from CacheStore for nodes in eval set, bump snapshot/version IDs, set Edit provenance, mark pending in cache for eval set nodes, build ValueMap from snapshot, clone graph/traces/reverse_index, return ConcurrentEditSetup.

Write failing test in reify-runtime tests/concurrent_eval.rs: ConcurrentEvalAdapter correctly evaluates a single value node. Build a simple topology (param a=5, let b = a * 2) using TopologyTemplateBuilder. Create ConcurrentEditSetup manually with values containing a=10, eval_set = [b]. Construct adapter, call evaluate(b_node).await. Verify: (1) adapter's shared values map now has b=20, (2) results vec contains one entry with outcome Changed, (3) adapter's shared snapshot_values has b entry with Determined.

Create ConcurrentEvalAdapter struct in reify-runtime/src/concurrent_eval.rs (new file). Fields: graph (Arc<EvaluationGraph>), values (Arc<std::sync::RwLock<ValueMap>>), snapshot_values (Arc<std::sync::RwLock<PersistentMap<ValueCellId, (Value, DeterminacyState)>>>), previous_hashes (Arc<HashMap<NodeId, ContentHash>>), reverse_index (Arc<ReverseDependencyIndex>), skipped (Arc<std::sync::RwLock<HashSet<NodeId>>>), results (Arc<std::sync::Mutex<Vec<ConcurrentNodeResult>>>), version (VersionId). Implement AsyncNodeEvaluator: is_dirty returns !skipped.contains(node); evaluate looks up expression from graph, calls reify_expr::eval_expr with read-locked values, writes result to values and snapshot_values, computes content hash, compares with previous_hashes for outcome, records ConcurrentNodeResult, returns outcome. Add constructor from_setup(setup: &ConcurrentEditSetup). Add pub mod concurrent_eval; to lib.rs.

Write failing test: edit_param_concurrent on a linear chain (param a → let b = a * 2) produces correct values. Build topology with builder, create Engine, eval(), then call edit_param_concurrent(engine, a_id, mm(50.0), cancel).await. Verify returned ConcurrentEditResult has correct values: a=50mm, b=100mm. Verify actual_eval_set contains NodeId::Value(b_id).

Implement pub async fn edit_param_concurrent(engine: &mut Engine, cell: ValueCellId, new_value: Value, cancel: &CancellationToken) -> Result<ConcurrentEditResult, SchedulerError> in concurrent_eval.rs. Steps: (1) call engine.prepare_concurrent_edit(cell, new_value), (2) create ConcurrentEvalAdapter from setup, wrap in Arc, (3) call ConcurrentScheduler.execute(eval_set, adapter, traces, cancel).await, (4) extract results from adapter into ConcurrentEditResult. Include into_result() method on adapter that consumes the Arc wrappers.

Write failing test: concurrent eval of 3 independent let bindings at the same topological level. Build topology: param a, let x = a+1, let y = a+2, let z = a+3. Change a from 5 to 10. All three lets are in dirty cone and eval_set at the same level. Verify all three values are correct (x=11, y=12, z=13) and all three appear in actual_eval_set and node_results.

Verify concurrent safety of the adapter when multiple nodes at the same level evaluate simultaneously. Each node acquires write lock on values briefly (insert), then releases. Since ValueCellIds are distinct across nodes in a level, there's no logical contention—only lock contention which is brief. Ensure tests pass reliably with tokio multi-threaded runtime.

Write failing test: multi-level diamond dependency. Build topology: param a, let b = a * 2, let c = a + 1, let d = b + c. Change a from 5 to 10. Levels: [b,c] then [d]. Verify d = 20 + 11 = 31 (not stale values). This validates that values written during level 0 (b=20, c=11) are visible to level 1 (d reads b and c).

Ensure multi-level value propagation: ConcurrentScheduler joins all level N handles before starting level N+1, so all write locks from level N are released and visible. The RwLock provides happens-before ordering. Verify the diamond test passes with correct d value computed from updated b and c.

Write failing test: early cutoff in concurrent mode. Build topology: param a, let x = a - a (always 0), let y = x + 1. Change a from 5 to 7. x is dirty, evaluates to 0 (same as before) → Unchanged. y depends on x only, should be skipped. Verify: (1) x appears in actual_eval_set with outcome Unchanged, (2) y is in skipped set, (3) y does NOT appear in node_results, (4) y retains its value of 1.0 in the result values map.

Implement early cutoff propagation in ConcurrentEvalAdapter::evaluate(). After computing outcome, if Unchanged: acquire write lock on skipped set, iterate reverse_index.dependents_of(vcid), insert each dependent into skipped. In is_dirty(): return !skipped.read().contains(node). ConcurrentScheduler checks is_dirty before spawning each node, so nodes added to skipped in level N won't be spawned in level N+1.

Write failing test: Engine::apply_concurrent_edit correctly updates Engine state. After edit_param_concurrent, call engine.apply_concurrent_edit(result). Verify: (1) cache_store has updated entries for each evaluated node with correct freshness=Final, result_hash matches, basis_version matches, (2) current snapshot values match the concurrent result values with Edit provenance, (3) last_eval_set() matches actual_eval_set, (4) journal has Started+Completed event pairs for each evaluated node with correct version, (5) skipped nodes have freshness restored to Final.

Implement Engine::apply_concurrent_edit(&mut self, setup: ConcurrentEditSetup, result: ConcurrentEditResult). For each ConcurrentNodeResult: call self.cache.record_evaluation(node, CachedResult::Value(value, det), version, trace). Record journal events: Started event (with Instant::now() for approximate timestamp) and Completed{outcome} event for each node result. Update self.current_snapshot with result's snapshot_values and the setup's snapshot metadata (id, version, provenance). For skipped nodes: call self.cache.restore_final(). Set self.last_eval_set = result.actual_eval_set. Build and return EvalResult from result.values.

Write failing test: cancellation stops concurrent evaluation between levels. Build topology: param a → let b (level 0), b → let c (level 1). Create a CancellationToken, trigger cancel inside a custom evaluator wrapper that cancels after evaluating b. Run edit_param_concurrent. Verify: (1) b appears in node_results, (2) c does NOT appear in node_results, (3) function returns Ok (scheduler's cooperative cancellation breaks between levels, not mid-task).

Verify cancellation integration: ConcurrentScheduler already checks cancel.is_cancelled() before each level and breaks. The adapter evaluates b, which triggers cancel. Before level 1, scheduler sees is_cancelled=true and breaks. edit_param_concurrent collects partial results from completed levels. Ensure ConcurrentEditResult correctly reflects only evaluated nodes.

Write integration test: bracket topology concurrent edit_param matches sequential. Use bracket_compiled_module(), two separate Engine instances with same MockConstraintChecker. Both: eval(module). Then: engine_seq.edit_param(width, mm(100.0)) vs edit_param_concurrent(engine_conc, width, mm(100.0), cancel). Compare: (1) all values match exactly, (2) both report same set of evaluated nodes (volume in both cases), (3) both produce same cache entries for volume (same content hash, same freshness).

Fix any remaining edge cases exposed by the bracket integration test. Ensure non-Value nodes (Constraint, Realization) in the eval set are tracked in actual_eval_set but not evaluated by the adapter (return Unchanged, matching sequential behavior). Verify Param nodes without expressions are handled correctly (no-op evaluation). Ensure concurrent EvalResult values match sequential EvalResult values exactly.

design_decisions.decision: State extraction pattern: Engine::prepare_concurrent_edit() + apply_concurrent_edit() instead of shared mutable Engine
design_decisions.rationale: Engine holds Box<dyn ConstraintChecker> and other non-Send types, making it impossible to share via Arc. The prepare/apply pattern extracts only the Clone-able, Send+Sync state needed for concurrent evaluation, keeping Engine's ownership model intact. PersistentMap makes cloning cheap (O(1) structural sharing for graph, values, snapshot_values).

design_decisions.decision: Pre-extract content hashes from CacheStore instead of sharing CacheStore with concurrent tasks
design_decisions.rationale: CacheStore uses HashMap (not concurrent-safe) and has complex dirty_reasons tracking. Sharing it via Mutex would create contention and complicate the API. Instead, extract HashMap<NodeId, ContentHash> from CacheStore before concurrent eval. The adapter computes Changed/Unchanged by comparing new hash with pre-extracted old hash. Batch-apply all results to the real CacheStore after concurrent eval completes via apply_concurrent_edit().

design_decisions.decision: Early cutoff via shared skip set (Arc<RwLock<HashSet<NodeId>>>) checked by is_dirty()
design_decisions.rationale: Mirrors the sequential edit_param() skipped-set approach. Within a topological level, nodes are independent so no intra-level early cutoff occurs. Between levels, ConcurrentScheduler joins all tasks before moving to next level, ensuring the skip set is fully populated from all Unchanged outcomes. The is_dirty() check before spawning each node in the next level correctly skips nodes whose upstream was Unchanged.

design_decisions.decision: ConcurrentEvalAdapter + edit_param_concurrent() live in reify-runtime; data types live in reify-eval
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps reify-eval free of tokio dependency. ConcurrentEditSetup and ConcurrentEditResult are plain data structs (no async) so they belong in reify-eval alongside Engine. The async adapter and orchestration function require tokio::spawn so they live in reify-runtime which already has the dependency.

design_decisions.decision: Use std::sync::RwLock (not tokio::sync::RwLock) for shared values during concurrent eval
design_decisions.rationale: Expression evaluation (eval_expr) is CPU-bound and completes in microseconds. Locks are held briefly for read (during eval) and write (to insert result). std::sync::RwLock avoids the overhead of async-aware locking and doesn't require awaiting. The lock is never held across .await points, so this is safe and correct in async context.

design_decisions.decision: Add reify-expr and reify-compiler as runtime dependencies of reify-runtime
design_decisions.rationale: ConcurrentEvalAdapter needs to call eval_expr (from reify-expr) and access CompiledExpr/ValueCellKind types (from reify-compiler) when looking up node expressions and determining cell kinds. reify-runtime is the integration layer crate, so depending on core evaluation crates is natural. reify-compiler was already a dev-dependency.

design_decisions.decision: Constraint/Realization/Resolution nodes tracked in eval_set but not evaluated during concurrent edit
design_decisions.rationale: Matches the sequential edit_param() behavior where only Value nodes with expressions are evaluated. Constraint checking and geometry building happen in separate check()/build() calls. The concurrent adapter returns Unchanged for non-Value nodes, and they appear in actual_eval_set for tracking purposes.

design_decisions.decision: Journal events recorded during apply phase with approximate timestamps, not during concurrent evaluation
design_decisions.rationale: Recording events during concurrent eval would require Arc<Mutex<EventJournal>> with contention across tasks. Instead, collect evaluation metadata (which nodes, outcomes, durations) in ConcurrentNodeResult, then replay as journal events during the synchronous apply phase. Timestamps are approximate but ordering is preserved.

reuse.how: Use directly as the async fan-out engine. It already handles level-based scheduling via compute_levels(), task spawning via tokio::spawn, joining, cancellation checking between levels, and SchedulerError propagation for panics. Just provide an AsyncNodeEvaluator implementation.

Implement for ConcurrentEvalAdapter. The trait's is_dirty/evaluate contract matches our skip-set + expression-eval pattern. evaluate takes NodeId by value (no lifetime issues with spawned tasks), returns impl Future<Output=EvalOutcome> + Send.
Pass through to ConcurrentScheduler::execute(). Already supports hierarchical cancellation via child_token(), cooperative checking via is_cancelled(), and async waiting via cancelled().

Reuse in prepare_concurrent_edit() to compute the eval set identically to the sequential path. dirty∩demand intersection with topological sorting produces the same eval_set for both sequential and concurrent execution.
Called from ConcurrentEvalAdapter::evaluate() to record dependency traces for each evaluated node, same as the sequential path.
Used in the adapter to compute the new content hash of each evaluation result and compare with pre-extracted previous_hashes for Changed/Unchanged determination.
Called from ConcurrentEvalAdapter::evaluate() to evaluate compiled expressions against current values. Same pure function as the sequential Engine path.
O(1) clone used in prepare_concurrent_edit() to snapshot current values. Wrapped in Arc<RwLock> for concurrent read/write access during evaluation.
Called during apply_concurrent_edit() to batch-update cache entries with concurrent evaluation results and restore freshness for skipped nodes.

Reuse for building test fixtures and topologies in concurrent evaluation tests. TopologyTemplateBuilder for custom test graphs (linear, diamond, fan-out). bracket_compiled_module for integration tests.
build_from_graph used in prepare phase to construct the index. dependents_of used by the adapter during early cutoff to find which downstream nodes to add to the skip set.
